description_long: This is a vintage-style boxcar train carriage with a rectangular, elongated body and a slightly curved, dark brown roof. The exterior is clad in horizontal wooden planks painted a weathered reddish-brown, giving it a rustic appearance. The sides feature large, rectangular openings, likely for loading and unloading cargo, with no visible glass or window frames. The undercarriage is equipped with four sets of black metal wheels and axles, typical of freight train cars. The ends of the carriage are flat and closed, with no visible doors or couplings. The overall design suggests it is intended for transporting goods rather than passengers.
description: A weathered, reddish-brown wooden boxcar with a curved dark roof and large side openings, mounted on four sets of black wheels. The carriage is designed for cargo transport and lacks visible windows or end doors.
description_view_0: The end view shows a flat, closed wooden panel with horizontal planks and no visible doors or windows.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the elongated body, large rectangular cargo opening, and four sets of black wheels beneath the reddish-brown wooden exterior.
description_view_2: The opposite end view is similar to the first, showing a flat, closed wooden panel with no openings.
description_view_3: The other side view mirrors the first side, with a large rectangular opening and the same wheel arrangement.
attribute_door_placement: large cargo openings on both sides, no visible end doors
